explanation: This command uses the original syntax and directly matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
Inserts quotes around the 'snapshot' flag, which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize quotes in command-line arguments.
Changes the flag from a short form to a long form equivalent, which may not be explicitly covered in the Sigma rule if it only checks for 'snapshot' as a substring.
Alters the case of the flag, which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize case in command-line arguments.
Uses an escape character (`) to split the 'snapshot' flag, which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize such characters.
Uses a placeholder for character insertion (e.g., range(3) as a simulated obfuscation technique), which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize such expressions.
Replaces part of the flag with its ASCII value (e.g., 'p' as 112), which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize encoded or obfuscated command-line arguments.
Uses command substitution to dynamically insert the 'snapshot' flag, which may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize or evaluate dynamic command-line content.
